commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=063c1c346d4ca022dbe026d5104c987922e64098)
commands.review_summary:
The branch carries source actor attribution through channel inbound context, embedded agent runs, plugin tool context, memory recall supplements, memory prompt guidance, docs, tests, and generated API baseline hashes.

PR surface: Source +261, Tests +396, Docs +41, Generated 0. Total +698 across 38 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows production context adds SourceActor and mention-decision fields while the public SDK runtime mock remains unchanged on this PR head. The central PR capability is a new feature, not a direct bug reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can update SDK mock parity; maintainer review still owns the public API shape and stack-ordering decision.

target.title: feat(memory): carry source actor context into recall
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Memory recall can currently receive the conversation/session context without a stable indication of which group participant authored the triggering message. In multi-person channels, that can make retrieved or promoted memory read as if another participant's facts belong to the operator. This PR carries the generic source actor context into memory recall and prompt supplements so memory backends can preserve participant attribution without adding backend-specific logic.

## Summary

- Carries `SourceActor` from inbound context through embedded runs and plugin tool context.
- Exposes the same source actor context to memory corpus supplements for `memory_search` and `memory_get`.
- Adds memory prompt/flush guidance to preserve participant attribution instead of merging another participant's facts into the operator/user profile.

## Stack

This is stacked after #96738, `codex/signal-mention-facts-context`, and `codex/source-actor-context-contract`. Until those land, this PR's diff will include its dependencies.

## Production impact

No storage migration and no backend-specific behavior. This is not Honcho-specific: it adds a generic runtime/tool/memory contract that memory backends and supplements can consume. Core memory search still filters by existing session/source visibility; this PR does not claim a new per-person storage partition.

## Evidence

- Agent/tool context tests cover `SourceActor` propagation into plugin tool context.
- Auto-reply and inbound metadata tests cover carrying source actor context through reply runs.
- Memory-core tests cover source actor supplements in recall/citation paths and attribution guidance in memory output.
- Broader affected-context regression suite passed:
  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.plugin-context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts extensions/memory-core/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.mention-gating.test.ts`
- Production TypeScript check passed:
  `pnpm tsgo:prod`

### Redacted Runtime Proof

Temporary local proof harness imported production context/tool/memory code and executed `memory_search`/`memory_get` through a registered corpus supplement on PR head `063c1c346d4c`.

```console
$ node --import tsx proof-runtime-source-actor.mts
runtime-proof:environment
{
  "repo": "openclaw/openclaw",
  "head": "063c1c346d4c",
  "node": "v26.3.0"
}
runtime-proof:#96863 source actor reaches plugin tool context
{
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:signal:group:<redacted>",
  "sourceActor": {
    "id": "signal:<redacted-phone>",
    "peerId": "signal:<redacted-uuid>",
    "displayName": "Kasia",
    "role": "participant",
    "context": "signal-group:<redacted>"
  }
}
runtime-proof:#96864 source_actor received by memory_search consumer
{
  "received": [
    {
      "query": "Kasia family context",
      "agentSessionKey": "agent:main:signal:group:<redacted>",
      "sourceActor": {
        "id": "signal:<redacted-phone>",
        "peerId": "signal:<redacted-uuid>",
        "displayName": "Kasia",
        "role": "participant",
        "context": "signal-group:<redacted>"
      },
      "corpus": "wiki"
    }
  ]
}
runtime-proof:#96864 source_actor received by memory_get consumer
{
  "received": [
    {
      "lookup": "entities/kasia.md",
      "agentSessionKey": "agent:main:signal:group:<redacted>",
      "sourceActor": {
        "id": "signal:<redacted-phone>",
        "peerId": "signal:<redacted-uuid>",
        "displayName": "Kasia",
        "role": "participant",
        "context": "signal-group:<redacted>"
      },
      "corpus": "all"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Tests

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.plugin-context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts extensions/memory-core/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.mention-gating.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.plugin-context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts extensions/memory-core/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.mention-gating.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
target.author: pdurlej
